Rate Limits

[Get Started](https://docs.moralis.com/) [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Auth API](https://docs.moralis.com/auth-api/overview) [Changelog](https://docs.moralis.com/changelog)

On this page

- [Throughput & Rolling Window](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/rate-limits#throughput-%26-rolling-window)
- [Example](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/rate-limits#example)
- [Maximum calls per second](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/rate-limits#maximum-calls-per-second)
- [Avoiding Rate Limits](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/rate-limits#avoiding-rate-limits)

Every API endpoint has a **rate limit cost** (CUs per request). Your plan defines how many CUs you can consume per second. If you exceed this, requests will be rate-limited.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/rate-limits\#throughput-&-rolling-window)  Throughput & Rolling Window

Throughput limits are evaluated over a **rolling 4-second window**, not per exact second.This allows short request bursts, as long as total CU usage stays within your plan’s limits over that window.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/rate-limits\#example)  Example

The `getWalletTokenBalancesPrice` endpoint has a rate limit cost of **20 CUs per call**:

```
{
  "endpoint": "getWalletTokenBalancesPrice",
  "path": "/wallets/{address}/tokens",
  "price": 100,
  "rateLimitCost": 20
}
```

#### [​](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/rate-limits\#maximum-calls-per-second)  Maximum calls per second

| Plan | Throughput | Max calls / second |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Free | 1,000 CU/s | 50 |
| Starter | 1,000 CU/s | 50 |
| Pro | 2,000 CU/s | 100 |
| Business | 5,000 CU/s | 250 |
| Enterprise | Custom | Custom / Unlimited |

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/rate-limits\#avoiding-rate-limits)  Avoiding Rate Limits

To avoid rate limiting:

- Keep total CU usage within your plan’s throughput
- Avoid sustained spikes that exceed the rolling 4-second limit
- Upgrade your plan if you consistently hit limits

If you exceed your throughput, the API will return a **429 Too Many Requests** response.

[Make Your First Request](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/first-request) [Pagination](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/resources/pagination)

web.title: Pricing - Moralis
web.description: All Moralis plans come with generous monthly request limits. The number of included requests depends on your plan - check the pricing page for details.
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#content-area)

[Moralis home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/light.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=66e6da0d81943c4679e0ba921850201d)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/dark.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=39f72ccb7cd088ee041f49d8b76acc8c)](https://docs.moralis.com/)

Navigation

Pricing & Resources

Pricing

[Get Started](https://docs.moralis.com/) [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Auth API](https://docs.moralis.com/auth-api/overview) [Changelog](https://docs.moralis.com/changelog)

On this page

- [What is a Compute Unit?](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#what-is-a-compute-unit)
- [CU Cost](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#cu-cost)
- [Dynamic Endpoints](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#dynamic-endpoints)
- [CU Throughput (CU/s)](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#cu-throughput-cu%2Fs)

All Moralis plans come with generous monthly request limits. The number of included requests depends on your plan - check the [pricing page](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#) for details.Moralis uses **Compute Units (CUs)** as a standard way to measure and bill for usage across all products - including [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview), [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview), [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview), and [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview).CUs provide a unified way to account for **computational complexity** and **rate limits**, regardless of the product you’re using.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing\#what-is-a-compute-unit)  What is a Compute Unit?

A **Compute Unit (CU)** represents the relative computational cost of a request, task, or operation within the Moralis platform. Simple operations consume fewer CUs, while more complex or resource-intensive ones consume more.For example:

- A basic wallet balance check may consume a small number of CUs.
- A multi-chain portfolio aggregation, or a high-frequency stream of on-chain events, may consume significantly more.

CUs ensure you’re only billed for the resources you actually use - and that high-load workloads are fairly metered across the system.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing\#cu-cost)  CU Cost

Each product applies CU-based billing differently. Generally:

- **CUs are deducted from your plan’s included quota**.
- **Usage beyond your quota may incur overage charges**, depending on your plan and product.

Visit the individual pricing pages to see how CUs apply to each product:

- [Data API Pricing](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/pricing)
- [Streams Pricing](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing)
- [Datashare Pricing](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing#)
- [Nodes Pricing](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing)

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing\#dynamic-endpoints)  Dynamic Endpoints

Some API endpoints have **dynamic CU costs** that scale based on the scope of the request. Instead of a fixed cost per call, these endpoints charge a base CU amount multiplied by a variable factor such as the number of chains, wallets, or addresses involved.For example:

- An endpoint costing **50 CUs per chain** that queries 3 chains will consume **150 CUs** total.
- An endpoint costing **250 CUs per chain** that queries 5 chains will consume **1,250 CUs** total.

Dynamic endpoints are clearly marked on their respective documentation pages. You can find CU costs for all endpoints on the individual product pricing pages listed above.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/pricing\#cu-throughput-cu/s)  CU Throughput (CU/s)

CUs are also used to enforce **rate limits**, measured in **CUs per second (CU/s)**.This helps manage request throughput and protect system performance - allowing heavier requests to be made less frequently, while lightweight calls remain fast and responsive.Different products may enforce CU/s limits differently. Check the respective documentation for details.

[Quickstart Guides](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/quickstart-guides) [Supported Chains](https://docs.moralis.com/get-started/supported-chains)

web.title: What are the limits for API requests? - Moralis
web.description: Our free-to-use Starter Plan currently has a limit of 1,000 Compute Units per second (CU/s) and 40,000 CU per day. Over this limit, free accounts are ...
web.markdown:
[Back to FAQ](https://moralis.com/faq/)

# Q:What are the limits for API requests?

A.

Our free-to-use Starter Plan currently has a limit of 1,000 Compute Units per second (CU/s) and 40,000 CU per day. Over this limit, free accounts are temporarily suspended until the beginning of the next day.

Our Pro Plan has a limit of 1,500 CU/s and 100M CU per month.

Our Business Plan has a limit of 5,000 CU/s and 500M CU per month.

If you go past the the included monthly quotas in Pro or Business, your app will continue to function and all additional requests will be billed according to our overage pricing.

If you need higher limits than the Pro or Business plans offers, feel free to reach out to [success@moralis.io](mailto:success@moralis.io) for an Enterprise or Custom solution.

## Streams New

### [What is the Number of Retries if Webhook Delivery Fails?](https://moralis.com/faq/what-is-the-number-of-retries-if-webhook-delivery-fails/)

### [What is the Failed Payload Retention Time?](https://moralis.com/faq/what-is-the-failed-payload-retention-time/)

### [How Many Internal Transactions Are Allowed?](https://moralis.com/faq/how-many-internal-transactions-are-allowed/)

### [Can I Listen to All Addresses?](https://moralis.com/faq/can-i-listen-to-all-addresses/)

### [Will I get charged for unconfirmed transactions?](https://moralis.com/faq/will-i-get-charged-for-unconfirmed-transactions/)

### [How many wallets can we use with the Streams API?](https://moralis.com/faq/how-many-wallets-can-we-use-with-the-streams-api/)

### [How many transactions/items are synced per wallet?](https://moralis.com/faq/how-many-transactions-items-are-synced-per-wallet/)

### [Does the Streams API provide historical data?](https://moralis.com/faq/does-the-streams-api-provide-historical-data/)

### [How many streams can a user have per account?](https://moralis.com/faq/how-many-streams-can-a-user-have-per-account/)

### [How many addresses can you add with “Moralis.Streams.addAddress”?](https://moralis.com/faq/how-many-addresses-can-you-add-with-moralis-streams-addaddress/)

### [Can there be a delay between the creation of the block and the webhook?](https://moralis.com/faq/can-there-be-a-delay-between-the-creation-of-the-block-and-the-webhook/)

### [Why do I receive 2 requests for the same transaction?](https://moralis.com/faq/why-do-i-receive-2-requests-for-the-same-transaction/)

### [After how many blocks is considered a transaction as confirmed?](https://moralis.com/faq/after-how-many-blocks-is-considered-a-transaction-as-confirmed/)

## Main Questions

### [What is Moralis?](https://moralis.com/faq/what-is-moralis/)

### [What does Moralis do?](https://moralis.com/faq/what-does-moralis-do/)

If you need higher limits than the Pro Plan offers, feel free to reach out to success@moralis.com for an Enterprise or Custom solution.

web.title: What happens if I exceed my monthly limit?
web.description: ​Free Plan - 40000 CU per day (When exceeded, the keys are automatically blocked until the next day.) ​ ​Pro Plan - 100M Compute Units per month (When ...
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://api-help.moralis.io/en/articles/19178-what-happens-if-i-exceed-my-monthly-limit#main-content)

# What happens if I exceed my monthly limit?

![](https://static.intercomassets.eu/avatars/50006295/square_128/TEXN16TFV-U02JRBP0UHW-31a93f6f4eb0-512-1666780763.jpg)

Written by Mauro Fabijanic
Updated over a year ago

It might be possible that you exceed your Monthly Compute Units limit on your current plan. To help you understand what the Monthly limits are for each plan and what to expect once it is reached, you will find below more details about the process:

​

​ **Free Plan** **\- 40000** CU per day ( _When exceeded, the keys are automatically blocked until the next day.)_

​

​ **Pro Plan - 100M** Compute Units per month (When exceeded, additional compute units are charged)

​

​ **Business Plan - 350M** Compute Units per month (When exceeded, additional compute units are charged)

​

​ **Enterprise Plan - 500M+** ( _For Enterprise plans, this might differ due to the custom plans and committed use discounts. You can always reach out to your Account Manager for more details about the Enterprise plan billing structure.)_

_​_

​

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.eu/i/o/12969495/bc7d2c24e8f74f13c8516352/image.png?expires=1776587400&signature=21cda3e5add84ed4bc3ab2c6a3ad6fdd3c31ea0fdc4dcfa14e278d8c37445012&req=0ddmw1H%2BpTJk2hL085ZhoWfqKvx3bebF5AK254wSkcH%2BFxjI56QMyBKB0tbK%0AIzeAZNrfb5FCyeCL%0A)

​

​

​

​ _Important Note: In the event that you are cancelling your subscription and you exceed the monthly request limit before the subscription ends, all additional requests accrued during that period will still be charged._

​

​

* * *

Related Articles

[Upgrade your plan (Increase Rate Limits)](https://api-help.moralis.io/en/articles/19158-upgrade-your-plan-increase-rate-limits) [Abuse Prevention](https://api-help.moralis.io/en/articles/19163-abuse-prevention) [What is a record? (Streams API)](https://api-help.moralis.io/en/articles/20681-what-is-a-record-streams-api) [Compute Units](https://api-help.moralis.io/en/articles/27695-compute-units) [New limits on the Free plan (As from 15th April 2023)](https://api-help.moralis.io/en/articles/64090-new-limits-on-the-free-plan-as-from-15th-april-2023)

Did this answer your question?

😞😐😃

web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#content-area)

[Moralis home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/light.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=66e6da0d81943c4679e0ba921850201d)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/dark.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=39f72ccb7cd088ee041f49d8b76acc8c)](https://docs.moralis.com/)

Search...

Ctrl KAsk AI

Search...

Navigation

Introduction

Pricing

[Get Started](https://docs.moralis.com/) [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Auth API](https://docs.moralis.com/auth-api/overview) [Changelog](https://docs.moralis.com/changelog)

On this page

- [What is a Compute Unit (CU)?](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#what-is-a-compute-unit-cu-)
- [Plan Tiers](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#plan-tiers)
- [Standard RPC Method Costs](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#standard-rpc-method-costs)
- [Extended RPC Method Costs](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#extended-rpc-method-costs)
- [Archive Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#archive-nodes)
- [Optimizing CU Usage](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#optimizing-cu-usage)
- [Need Higher Limits?](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing#need-higher-limits)

All [Moralis plans](https://moralis.io/pricing) include generous monthly limits on the number of requests you can make. Some requests are more computationally expensive than others, so heavier requests are assigned higher **Compute Unit (CU) weights**.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#what-is-a-compute-unit-cu-)  What is a Compute Unit (CU)?

A **Compute Unit** is a measure of computational load assigned to each RPC request. Each method has a **CU Weight** that is deducted from your total available CUs. Archive requests (queries to historical data) often have a higher **Archive CU Weight**.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#plan-tiers)  Plan Tiers

|  | Starter | Pro | Business | Enterprise |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CUs Included | 10,000,000 | 100,000,000 | 350,000,000 | Custom |
| API Throughput (CUs/second) | 1,500 | 1,500 | 3,000 | Custom |
| Node Throughput (CUs/second) | 100 | 100 | 300 | Custom |
| Number of Nodes | 2 | 5 | Unlimited | Unlimited |
| Archive Requests | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#standard-rpc-method-costs)  Standard RPC Method Costs

| RPC Method | CU Weight | Archive CU Weight |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `eth_blockNumber` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_call` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_chainId` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_createAccessList` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_estimateGas` | 5 | 5 |
| `eth_feeHistory` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_gasPrice` | 3 | 3 |
| `eth_getBalance` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockByHash` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockByNumber` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockTransactionCountByHash` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockTransactionCountByNumber` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getBlockReceipts` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getCode` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getLogs` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getProof` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getStorageAt` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getTransactionByHash` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getTransactionByBlockHashAndIndex` | 12 | 12 |
| `eth_getTransactionByBlockNumberAndIndex` | 3 | 12 |
| `eth_getTransactionCount` | 2 | 8 |
| `eth_getTransactionReceipt` | 8 | 8 |
| `eth_getUncleByBlockHashAndIndex` | 8 | 8 |
| `eth_getUncleByBlockNumberAndIndex` | 2 | 8 |
| `eth_getUncleCountByBlockHash` | 8 | 8 |
| `eth_getUncleCountByBlockNumber` | 2 | 8 |
| `eth_maxPriorityFeePerGas` | 2 | 2 |
| `eth_sendRawTransaction` | 3 | 3 |

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#extended-rpc-method-costs)  Extended RPC Method Costs

Moralis provides extended RPC methods that offer advanced data retrieval. These are more computationally intensive and have higher CU weights.

| Method | Description | CU Weight |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `eth_getTransactions` | Get native transactions by wallet address | 15 |
| `eth_getDecodedTransactions` | Get decoded wallet history | 30 |
| `eth_getTokenBalances` | Get ERC20 token balances by wallet | 25 |
| `eth_getTokenPrice` | Get ERC20 token price by token address | 25 |
| `eth_getTokenMetadata` | Get ERC20 token metadata by token address | 8 |
| `eth_getNFTBalances` | Get NFTs by wallet address | 20 |
| `eth_getNFTCollections` | Get NFT collections by wallet address | 20 |

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#archive-nodes)  Archive Nodes

Archive nodes store historical blockchain data beyond the most recent blocks. Because of the additional computational and storage resources required, querying archive nodes comes with a higher CU cost. If your use case involves accessing deep historical data, be mindful of the increased cost shown in the **Archive CU Weight** column above.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#optimizing-cu-usage)  Optimizing CU Usage

- **Limit block range** — The default block range for queries is 100 blocks. Requesting larger ranges or querying archive data increases CU consumption.
- **Monitor usage** — Track your CU usage in the [Moralis Dashboard](https://admin.moralis.com/) to avoid hitting rate limits.
- **Use batch requests** — Combine multiple requests into a single API call. Note that batching does not reduce total CU cost, but it reduces HTTP overhead. Maximum batch size is 20 requests per call. See [Batch Requests](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-batch-requests) for details.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/pricing\#need-higher-limits)  Need Higher Limits?

If your use case requires more CUs than your current plan offers, consider upgrading or reaching out to the Moralis team for custom solutions at [moralis.io/pricing](https://moralis.io/pricing).

[Overview](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Supported Chains](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/supported-chains)

Ctrl+I

Assistant

Responses are generated using AI and may contain mistakes.

web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing#content-area)

[Moralis home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/light.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=66e6da0d81943c4679e0ba921850201d)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/dark.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=39f72ccb7cd088ee041f49d8b76acc8c)](https://docs.moralis.com/)

Search...

Ctrl KAsk AI

Search...

Navigation

Introduction

Pricing

[Get Started](https://docs.moralis.com/) [Data API](https://docs.moralis.com/data-api/overview) [Streams](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) [Datashare](https://docs.moralis.com/datashare/overview) [Data Indexer](https://docs.moralis.com/data-indexer/overview) [RPC Nodes](https://docs.moralis.com/rpc-nodes/overview) [Auth API](https://docs.moralis.com/auth-api/overview) [Changelog](https://docs.moralis.com/changelog)

On this page

- [Records](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing#records)
- [Charging Structure](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing#charging-structure)
- [Records by Transaction Type](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing#records-by-transaction-type)
- [Decoded Logs Are Free](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing#decoded-logs-are-free)
- [Monitoring Your Usage](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing#monitoring-your-usage)
- [Plan Limits](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing#plan-limits)

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing\#records)  Records

Records are the fundamental unit for calculating Streams API usage. A **record** is one of the following:

- A transaction (`txs`)
- A log event (`logs`)
- An internal transaction (`txsInternal`)

The total record count for a webhook equals the sum of all three: **txs + logs + txsInternal**.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing\#charging-structure)  Charging Structure

Each record costs **10 Compute Units (CUs)**. The `x-records-charged` header in webhook responses shows the exact record count for that delivery.

Only webhooks with `confirmed: true` incur charges. Unconfirmed webhooks (`confirmed: false`) have `x-records-charged: 0` and are free.

For each transaction, you receive two webhooks:

1. **Unconfirmed** — Sent when the transaction is included in a block (free).
2. **Confirmed** — Sent once the block is considered final (charged).

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing\#records-by-transaction-type)  Records by Transaction Type

The number of records charged varies depending on transaction complexity:

| Transaction Type | Records Charged |
| --- | --- |
| Native transfer | 1 record |
| ERC20 transfer | 2 records |
| Single NFT transfer (ERC721) | 11 records |
| Batch NFT transfer (ERC1155) | 2 records |
| ERC721 minting (100 tokens) | 100 records |

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing\#decoded-logs-are-free)  Decoded Logs Are Free

Moralis automatically decodes standardized contract events at **no additional cost**. These do **not** count as records:

- `erc20Transfers`
- `erc20Approvals`
- `nftTransfers`

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing\#monitoring-your-usage)  Monitoring Your Usage

Use the `/status` endpoint ( [Get Stats](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/api-reference/stats/get-stats)) to track your consumption. It provides:

- `totalLogsProcessed`
- `totalTxsProcessed`
- `totalTxsInternalProcessed`

Sum these values to determine total records consumed during your billing period.

## [​](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/pricing\#plan-limits)  Plan Limits

For details on CU allocations, throughput limits, and plan comparisons, visit the [Moralis Pricing Page](https://moralis.io/pricing).

[Overview](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/overview) [Supported Chains](https://docs.moralis.com/streams/supported-chains)

Ctrl+I

Assistant

Responses are generated using AI and may contain mistakes.

[Moralis home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/light.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=66e6da0d81943c4679e0ba921850201d)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/moralis/GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO/logo/dark.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=GWFD1O4mIZzaStPO&q=85&s=39f72ccb7cd088ee041f49d8b76acc8c)](https://moralis.mintlify.app/)

Search...

Ctrl KAsk AI

Search...

Navigation

Resources

RPC Rate Limits

[Get Started](https://moralis.mintlify.app/) [Data API](https://moralis.mintlify.app/data-api/overview) [Streams](https://moralis.mintlify.app/streams/overview) [Datashare](https://moralis.mintlify.app/datashare/overview) [Data Indexer](https://moralis.mintlify.app/data-indexer/overview) [RPC Nodes](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/overview) [Auth API](https://moralis.mintlify.app/auth-api/overview) [Changelog](https://moralis.mintlify.app/changelog)

On this page

- [Overview](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits#overview)
- [Rate Limits by Plan](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits#rate-limits-by-plan)
- [Understanding Throughput](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits#understanding-throughput)
- [Avoiding Rate Limits](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits#avoiding-rate-limits)
- [Rate Limit Response](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits#rate-limit-response)
- [Need Higher Limits?](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits#need-higher-limits)

## [​](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits\#overview)  Overview

Moralis uses Compute Units (CUs) to track API usage. Rate limits ensure fair usage and system stability across all users.

## [​](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits\#rate-limits-by-plan)  Rate Limits by Plan

| Plan | CUs Included | API Throughput | Node Throughput | Nodes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Starter | 10M | 1,500 CU/s | 100 CU/s | 2 |
| Pro | 100M | 1,500 CU/s | 100 CU/s | 5 |
| Business | 350M | 3,000 CU/s | 300 CU/s | Unlimited |
| Enterprise | Custom | Custom | Custom | Unlimited |

All plans include archive request support.

## [​](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits\#understanding-throughput)  Understanding Throughput

- **API Throughput** \- Maximum CUs per second across all your requests
- **Node Throughput** \- Maximum CUs per second per individual node
- **Nodes** \- Number of dedicated node endpoints available

## [​](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits\#avoiding-rate-limits)  Avoiding Rate Limits

To stay within limits:

1. Monitor your CU consumption in the dashboard
2. Keep total CU/s below your plan’s throughput
3. Implement exponential backoff for retries
4. Cache responses when possible
5. Use batch requests to reduce HTTP overhead (not CU cost)

## [​](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits\#rate-limit-response)  Rate Limit Response

When rate limited, you’ll receive an HTTP 429 response:

```
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "error": {
    "code": -32005,
    "message": "Rate limit exceeded"
  },
  "id": 1
}
```

## [​](https://moralis.mintlify.app/rpc-nodes/introduction/resources/rpc-rate-limits\#need-higher-limits)  Need Higher Limits?
